scope.focus: Resolve the dynamic caller/wrapper-propagated request-prep stack argument 7 paths before the byte is stored into controller +0xB8.

confirmed_field_role.writer: 0x14015459F loads request-prep stack argument 7 from [RSP+0x80], and 0x1401545A7 stores AL into controller +0xB8.
confirmed_field_role.consumer: 0x140154CD5..0x140154CE7 reads +0xB8 only when len(controller +0xA0) == 6. Nonzero selects packet selector 1; zero selects packet selector 3.
confirmed_field_role.negative_result: +0xB8 is not the cached TOTP map lookup result. The selector-0 map path writes +0xB9/+0xBC separately.

source_classes.meaning: The request-prep +0xB8 byte is forced nonzero, so a six-character auth code emits selector 1.
The request-prep +0xB8 byte is forced zero, so a six-character auth code emits selector 3.
The request-prep +0xB8 byte is set with SETZ after comparing a per-slot dword to 1; equal selects selector 1, not equal selects selector 3.
The request-prep +0xB8 byte is set with SETZ after comparing [RBX+0x104] to 1; equal selects selector 1, not equal selects selector 3.

resolved_cases.evidence: Callsite register artifact shows stack argument 5 is immediate zero.
Callsite register artifact shows stack argument 5 is immediate one.

resolved_cases.selector_when_len_6: 1 when the compared slot equals 1, otherwise 3
resolved_cases.evidence: 0x1401F5370 compares [R8+RAX*4+0x100] to 1; 0x1401F537D SETZ SIL; SIL is passed as stack argument 5.

resolved_sources.important_boundary_note: 0x140153A16 is a .pdata split/fallthrough boundary with no direct branch target. The real callable entry is 0x1401539B0.

resolved_cases.selector_when_len_6: 1 when the compared slot equals 1, otherwise 3
resolved_cases.evidence: 0x1401F5A8A compares [R8+RAX*4+0x100] to 1; 0x1401F5A97 SETZ BL; BL is copied into R8D before calling 0x1401539B0.

resolved_sources.resolved_source: incoming R9B copied into DIL, or forwarded through 0x1401536B0 from incoming R8B

resolved_cases.evidence: 0x1401536B0 copies incoming R8B into DIL and forwards it as R9D; 0x140026F25 passes R8D = 0.
0x1401536B0 copies incoming R8B into DIL and forwards it as R9D; 0x1401AD06C passes R8D = 0.

resolved_cases.selector_when_len_6: 1 when the compared slot equals 1, otherwise 3
resolved_cases.evidence: 0x1401F55EA compares [R8+RAX*4+0x100] to 1; 0x1401F55F7 SETZ BL; BL is copied into R8D before calling 0x1401536B0.

opennxt_947_impact: A six-character auth code has at least two packet forms. The server must decode selector 1 and selector 3 independently instead of assuming the six-character string alone determines the packet shape.
Several launcher/login paths force selector 3; others force selector 1 or select dynamically from auth/session slot state.
The repeated [R8+RAX*4+0x100] == 1 pattern is now a concrete 947 compatibility target for OpenNXT tests and server-side auth fixture coverage.
The cached selector-0 path remains separate and should continue to be mapped through controller +0xB9/+0xBC.
next_targets: Name the object behind [R8+RAX*4+0x100] and connect the index source back to the login/session table.
Continue mapping the upstream source that becomes controller +0xBC for selector 0.
Add OpenNXT 947 decoder fixtures for selector 1 and selector 3 once packet byte examples are available.
Fold this repeated callsite-source trace pattern into the Reverser tool if another selector field needs the same analysis.
